title: Project Pulse
summary: The continuity baseline is now merged on main, and the active NEXUS engineering lane has shifted to the first native-runtime topology pass in issue #57.
source: GitHub-first NEXUS truth sweep after PR #58 merge and issue #54 closure.
nextAction: Define the first concrete host/runtime split for the native runtime, Avalonia host, interop boundary, and child-surface packaging while keeping the Electron baseline explicit as the migration source.

lanes.title: Project Pulse operator surface
lanes.summary: The right-rail Project Pulse and readable runtime-health surface shipped to main and now anchor operator-visible progress and readiness in the client.
lanes.nextAction: Keep the snapshot truthful as active NEXUS lanes move.

lanes.title: Desktop readiness validation
lanes.summary: Continuity validation closed cleanly after the current Electron and Node baseline passed 72 out of 72 automated checks and the operator-readability slice landed.
lanes.nextAction: Use the verified baseline as the migration source for the next runtime-topology work.

lanes.title: Packaging and runtime topology
lanes.summary: Native-C-first topology is now the active engineering lane, with Avalonia host responsibilities, native runtime ownership, and interop boundaries needing explicit first-pass definition.
lanes.nextAction: Define the first concrete host/runtime split and the package boundaries for channel, forum, thread, timeline, and direct surfaces.

lanes.title: README direction clarification
lanes.summary: The README clarification lane remains open so the current Electron baseline and the target native-C plus Avalonia direction stay explicitly separated.
lanes.nextAction: Land the clarification without mixing issue-57 topology work back into the README-only lane.

lanes.title: Conversation-surface program model
lanes.summary: The child-program and embedded-helper surface model is queued behind the continuity-validation and runtime-topology work already in flight.
lanes.nextAction: Unblock this lane after the first runtime-topology split is recorded.

lanes.title: ASCENT shared-surface placeholder
lanes.summary: Reserved integration space only. This remains intentionally parked and should not pull focus away from the current continuity and runtime lanes.
lanes.nextAction: Leave parked until the active desktop and conversation-surface lanes warrant reopening it.

lanes.title: ASCENT notification placeholder
lanes.summary: Reserved notification integration space only. It stays parked as a placeholder rather than active build work.
lanes.nextAction: Keep parked until the current NEXUS execution lanes are materially further along.
